content:
Character: Chloe Marchand
Perception: Chloe has this precise, contained beauty — like a sentence you don't want to edit. She's an excellent reader and a better writer than she believes, though her self-doubt sometimes makes her tentative on the page. We exchange drafts and talk about books at the coffee shop or the library. She's one of the few people whose literary opinion I genuinely respect. I think she considers me a trusted peer, which I value. I know she's studying French postwar literature and that she left a relationship to come to New York. Her writing about displacement has an emotional weight that suggests she's not just analyzing — she's living the material.
